DesiredReplicas.description: Configure the expected number of nodes in the node pool

Enabled.description: Configure the node pool auto scaling feature switch. Parameter values: false: disabled. true: enabled.

MaxReplicas.description: Configure the maximum number of nodes in the node pool

MinReplicas.description: Minimum number of nodes configured for the node pool

SubnetPolicy.description: Multi-subnet scheduling strategy for the node pool. Parameter values: ZoneBalance: availability zone balancing strategy. Priority: subnet priority strategy.

Type.description: Status conditions for the node pool in the current main state, i.e., the reasons for entering this main state. There can be multiple reasons. Parameter values: ProgressingOk, ResourceCleanupFailed, Unknown, ClusterNotRunning.

InsecureRegistries.description: Specify the container image repository address to skip certificate verification.

MountPoint.description: Target mount directory after disk formatting.

SnapshotId.description: Create a data disk from a snapshot. You can call the DescribeSnapshots API to query the snapshot ID. Note: Only Ultra SSD and High-performance Cloud Disk support creating disks from snapshots. Only snapshots in the 'available' status can be used to create new disks. Snapshots in 'creating', 'rolling back', 'deleting', or 'error' status cannot be used to create new disks.

Type.description: Disk type: ESSD_PL0: ultra-fast SSD disk with performance level PL0. ESSD_FlexPL: ultra-fast SSD disk with performance level PL1

EvictionHard.description: Set of hard thresholds for triggering Pod eviction

Key.description: Hard threshold name. Options: memory.available, nodefs.available, nodefs.inodesFree, imagefs.available

InPlacePodVerticalScaling.description: Enable local vertical Pod autoscaling feature toggle

QoSResourceManager.description: Whether to enable the QoSResourceManager feature switch. Values: - true enabled; - false disabled

CapacityRebalance.description: Capacity rebalancing. Value range: - true: enables this feature. When a preemptible instance is about to be reclaimed, proactively creates a new preemptible instance for compensation. - false (default): disables this feature. Waits until the preemptible instance is reclaimed before scaling out to make up the instance count

CompensateWithOnDemand.description: On-demand instance fallback feature. Value range: - true: enable this feature; when all preemptible instances fail to purchase due to insufficient inventory or other reasons, attempt to purchase on-demand instances - false (default): disable this feature; only attempt to purchase configured preemptible instances when scaling up

OnDemandBaseCapacity.description: Size of the base capacity. The base capacity is always pay-as-you-go instances. Value range: [0,500], default is 0.

OnDemandPercentageAboveBaseCapacity.description: Proportion of pay-as-you-go instances in the part exceeding the base capacity. Value range: [0, 100]. 0 means only preemptible instances are produced for the part exceeding the base capacity; 100 means only pay-as-you-go instances are produced. Default value is 0.

Name.description: Resource name. Valid values: cpu or memory.

Quantity.description: Specify the resource quantity. - For cpu, example value: 200m - For memory, example value: 1G

CpuManagerPolicy.description: Configure kubelet CpuManagerPolicy. Supports none and static policies

EvictionHard.description: Set of hard thresholds for triggering Pod eviction

KubeApiBurst.description: Maximum burst request rate sent to the API server per second. Excludes events and node heartbeat APIs, whose rate limits are controlled by a different set of flags

KubeApiQps.description: Queries per second (QPS) for communication with apiserver. Events and node heartbeat APIs are not included; their rate limits are controlled by a different set of flags.

KubeReserved.description: Resources reserved for Kubernetes system components on the node. By default, half of the default value in the node reserved resource policy is reserved.

MaxPods.description: Configure the maximum number of Pods supported by kubelet

RegistryBurst.description: Set the maximum number of burst image pulls. Temporarily allows the number of image pulls specified by this parameter, provided it does not exceed the RegistryPullQps setting

RegistryPullQps.description: Used to limit the QPS cap for the image repository

SystemReserved.description: Resources reserved for the operating system on the node. By default, half of the default value in the node reserved resource policy is used for resource reservation.

TopologyManagerPolicy.description: Topology management policy. Values: none (default): disables topology management policy. restricted: kubelet only accepts Pods that achieve optimal NUMA (Non-Uniform Memory Access) on requested resources. best-effort: kubelet prioritizes Pods that achieve NUMA on CPU and device resources. single-numa-node: kubelet only allows Pods that achieve NUMA on CPU and device resources within the same node

TopologyManagerScope.description: Resource granularity for topology management policy. Values: container: resource alignment at container level pod: resource alignment at Pod level

AutoSyncDisabled.description: Disable automatic synchronization of labels and taints to existing nodes. Parameter values: true: disable (turn off automatic sync). false: do not disable (turn on automatic sync)

Cordon.description: Node blocking configuration. Parameter values: false: not blocked; true: blocked

Labels.description: Kubernetes label information for node pools/nodes

NamePrefix.description: Prefix for node object metadata name in Kubernetes

NameSuffix.description: Suffix for node object metadata name in Kubernetes

NameUseHostname.description: Whether the node object metadata name in Kubernetes uses the ECS host name. Values: true: use ECS host name as node name; false: do not use ECS host name as node name

Taints.description: Kubernetes taint information for node pools/nodes

Label.description: Kubernetes label information for node pools/nodes

Password.description: Root user login password in Base64 encoding. Follow the cloud server's requirements for instance passwords: 8–30 characters in length, must not start with / or $6$, supports the following character types and must include at least three types: lowercase letters a–z, uppercase letters A–Z, numbers 0–9, special characters ( ) ` ~ ! @ # $ % ^ & * _ - + = | { } [ ] : ; ' < > , . ? /

SshKeyPairName.description: SSH key pair name. Ensure the key pair is already created or hosted in the cloud server

Type.description: Node access login method. Parameter values: Password: password login; SshKeyPair: SSH key pair login

Enabled.description: Enable managed mode for node pool. Options: - true: on - false: off

NodeConfig.description: Cloud server instance configuration in the node pool

AdditionalContainerStorageEnabled.description: Whether the first data disk of the node is configured, formatted, and mounted as the storage directory for container images and logs. Parameter values: false: not configured and formatted; true: configured and formatted

AutoRenew.description: Whether the cloud server instance will be automatically renewed upon expiration. Parameter description: true: auto renewal false: no auto renewal This parameter is returned only when InstanceChargeType=PrePaid.

AutoRenewPeriod.description: Automatic renewal duration for cloud server instances, in months. This parameter is returned only when AutoRenew=true

DeploymentSetGroupNumber.description: Group number of the instance in the deployment set; 0 means not set

DeploymentSetId.description: Deployment Set ID to be added to the instance

Hostname.description: The host name corresponding to the node.

HpcClusterIds.description: High performance computing cluster ID. When the node pool is configured with high performance computing GPU node types, returns the high performance computing cluster ID

ImageId.description: Image ID used by the cloud server corresponding to the node

InitializeScript.description: Custom script executed after node creation and initialization. Shell script in Base64 encoding

InstanceChargeType.description: Cloud server instance billing type. Parameter values: PostPaid: pay-as-you-go. PrePaid: subscription (annual/monthly).

InstanceTypeIds.description: List of cloud server instance type IDs corresponding to the nodes

InstancesDistribution.description: Set the mixed strategy for spot and pay-as-you-go instances. This strategy takes effect when SpotStrategy is SpotAsPriceGo.

NamePrefix.description: Node name prefix. An empty string or nil means the node name prefix policy is not enabled.

Period.description: Purchase duration for cloud server instances, in months. This parameter is returned only when InstanceChargeType=PrePaid

PreScript.description: Script to execute before deploying the node

ProjectName.description: Project to which the ECS belongs. Each ECS can only belong to one project.

PublicAccessConfig.description: Configuration for automatic public network access for nodes

PublicAccessEnabled.description: Enable public network access for nodes automatically. Options: - false: off - true: on

SpotStrategy.description: Spot pricing strategy for pay-as-you-go billing. When InstanceChargeType=PostPaid: NoSpot: Standard pay-as-you-go instance. SpotAsPriceGo: System automatically bids, following the current market price.

SubnetIds.description: List of subnet IDs for the node network.

SystemVolume.description: System disk configuration for the node

Tags.description: Label information configured for the node pool

CreatingCount.description: Total number of nodes in Phase=Creating.

DeletingCount.description: Total number of nodes with Phase=Deleting

FailedCount.description: Total number of nodes with Phase=Failed

RunningCount.description: Total number of nodes with Phase=Running

TotalCount.description: Total number of nodes in the node pool

UpdatingCount.description: Total number of nodes with Phase=Updating

PublicAccessConfig.description: Configuration for automatic public network access for nodes

Bandwidth.description: Peak bandwidth for public IP, unit: Mbps

BillingType.description: Billing type for public IP: 2: pay-as-you-go by bandwidth cap. 3: pay-as-you-go by actual traffic.

Isp.description: Public IP line type. Parameter description: BGP: BGP (multi-line)

SecurityGroupIds.description: List of security group IDs for the node network

SecurityStrategies.description: Node security policy. Parameter description: Hids: Host security hardening.

SecurityStrategyEnabled.description: Whether the node has enabled security hardening configuration. Parameter values: true: enabled; false: not enabled

Conditions.description: Status conditions for the current primary state of the node pool, i.e., the reason for entering this primary state

Phase.description: Status of the node pool. Parameter values: Creating, Running, Updating, Deleting, Failed, Scaling

Name.description: Resource name. Valid values: cpu or memory.

Quantity.description: Resource quantity for the specified resource. - For cpu, example value: 200m. - For memory, example value: 1G.

Size.description: Disk capacity, in GiB. Value description: Default: 40 GiB. Ultra-fast SSD (ESSD_PL0, ESSD_FlexPL): 40–2048 GiB

Type.description: Cloud disk types: ESSD_PL0: (default) Ultra-fast SSD cloud disk with performance level PL0. ESSD_FlexPL: Ultra-fast SSD cloud disk with performance level PL1.

Effect.description: Taint effect. Values: NoSchedule: (default) Do not schedule. NoExecute: Evict pods that do not tolerate the taint. PreferNoSchedule: Prefer not to schedule.

description: A node pool is a group of nodes in a cluster with the same configuration. A node pool can contain one or more nodes. The node pool configuration includes node attributes such as node specifications, availability zones, labels, taints, and more. These attributes can be specified when creating the node pool or edited after creation.

ClusterId.description: ID of the cluster where the node pool is located

KubernetesConfig.description: Kubernetes-related configuration for the node pool

Name.description: Node pool name. The node pool name must be unique within the same cluster. Supports uppercase and lowercase English letters, Chinese characters, numbers, and hyphens (-). Length limit: 2–64 characters.

NodeConfig.description: Cloud server (ECS) instance configuration in the node pool

NodeStatistics.description: Node statistics in the node pool.

RetainResources.description: When deleting a node pool, specify related resources to retain. Values: Empty value (default): delete associated resources Ecs: when deleting the node pool, retain ECS instances in the node pool. If the node pool contains prepaid ECS instances and retaining ECS is not selected, this API only removes the ECS instance from the node pool and does not delete it. You can query the instance via the ECS console or ECS API and perform subsequent operations as needed.
